persona.name: Lucia Vega

occupation.title: Head Stylist and Salon Manager
occupation.organization: Studio Marisol (independent full-service hair salon), Marietta, GA
occupation.description: Co-owns and manages a 6-chair salon in Marietta, handling both client-facing hair services and day-to-day business operations. On her feet 8–9 hours per day on hard floors. Her aesthetic calibration is sharp and professional — she notices quality and presentation in everything around her. Shoes are both a practical necessity and a visible statement of professional identity.

persona.education: Cosmetology certification, Atlanta Institute of Cosmetology; business management coursework at Kennesaw State University
long_term_goals: Open a second salon location within 4 years
Keep her body able to work on her feet well into her 50s
Put her daughter Sofia through college
Travel to Medellín with Carlos for their 20th anniversary
persona.style: Warm, expressive, and socially confident — reads a room quickly and adjusts her register accordingly. Blends professional composure with personal candor; clients feel like friends within 20 minutes. Visually attuned in a way that's both professional training and natural sensibility. Has a firmly established personal style built over 15 years: clean, put-together, approachable. Occasionally mixes Spanish into comfortable conversation without thinking about it.

traits: High social energy — naturally connects with people across ages and backgrounds
Practically aesthetic — wants things to look good and work reliably
Budget-conscious in a focused way: will spend on quality where it matters, not on hype
Protective of her time and body as business assets
Community-oriented — her salon is a neighborhood gathering point

interests: Athletic streetwear that transitions from work to errands without changing
Latin dance classes — salsa and merengue on Thursday evenings
Cooking Colombian food: bandeja paisa, arepas, sancocho
Following fashion and beauty influencers on Instagram
Light jogging two mornings per week
Keeping up with Sofia's school and activities
Watching novelas in the evenings as wind-down
likes: Sneakers that look clean enough to wear through a full salon day
Adidas Ultraboost and Nike Air Zoom Pegasus for all-day standing support
White or neutral colorways that stay sharp against dark salon floors
Good arch support — non-negotiable after years of standing work
Peer recommendations from other stylists on what holds up
Sales that make quality shoes accessible without waiting for paycheck timing
dislikes: Shoes that scuff or look cheap after 6 weeks
'Athletic nerd' aesthetics — over-engineered technical look with no visual ease
Trying too hard — shoes with too many design elements that cancel each other out
Narrow shoes that are painful by hour 5 on the floor
Spending over $160 without a very clear reason

beliefs: How you present yourself is part of how seriously people take your work
Value isn't about price — it's about what you get for what you pay over time
Family and community come before trends
You can't do good work if your body is in pain — that's just reality
skills: Hair styling and color (specialist in natural texture and Dominican blowout)
Salon operations and small business management
Style and presentation consulting for clients — informal but effective
Budget planning for household and business

general: Jogs twice a week early morning before the salon opens — consistency matters more than pace
Buys shoes based on other stylists' recommendations and what she sees on clients she respects
Evaluates shoes first visually (does it read clean?), second by comfort (can she stand in it for 9 hours?), third by price
Will not buy something that scuffed easily in a review — floor environments are punishing

morning: Shower, coffee, and getting Sofia ready before leaving at 8:30am
workday: Admin and scheduling in breaks between clients
evening: Family dinner with Carlos and Sofia
Dance class Thursday — the one thing that's just for her
Novelas and light social media scroll before bed
weekend: Salon closed Sunday; slow family morning
Grocery shopping and meal prep Saturday afternoon
Church or family gathering most Sundays

health.physical: Managing mild bunions from years of narrow footwear before she knew better. Now strictly wide-toe-box shoes only. Generally healthy; the jogging and dancing maintain her cardiovascular health. Back tension from standing hours addressed with a chiropractor once a month.
health.mental: Thrives in social environments — the salon fills her, not drains her. Stress comes from business decisions, not the work itself. Strong family support network. Occasional overwhelm when school pickup, client scheduling, and supplier issues stack on the same day.

relationships.name: Carlos Vega
relationships.description: Construction contractor who manages three crews. Shares Lucia's practical orientation; they make major purchases together and rarely disagree on financial logic.

relationships.name: Sofia Vega
relationships.description: 14 years old, already very interested in fashion. Asks Lucia about shoes for school with regularity; Lucia takes the questions seriously.

relationships.name: Elena
relationships.description: Has been coming to the salon for 8 years. They have become genuine friends. Elena recommended the Ultraboosts that Lucia currently wears to work.
